title: Feed Discovery, URL Identity, Scope, and SSRF Boundary · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs feed discovery, url identity, scope, and ssrf boundary from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for authorized feed discovery and network-scope policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Distinguish a direct feed URL from HTML autodiscovery and require an explicit bounded discovery policy.
Admit only alternate links with supported RSS or Atom media types, resolve references against the response URL, and bound candidates.
Normalize only justified URI components while preserving distinctions that can identify different publisher resources.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Discovery Provenance
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Discovery Provenance: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 5694 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded feed discovery, URL identity, SSRF decisions, and provenance; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain submitted URL, response URL, selected candidate, redirect chain, resolution evidence, and operator decision.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Discovery Provenance.
steps.why: Retrieving Feed Go Discovery Provenance through the concrete authorized feed discovery and network-scope policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Discovery Provenance, keep Feed Go Discovery Provenance connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retain submitted URL, response URL, selected candidate, redirect chain, resolution evidence, and operator decision.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain submitted URL, response URL, selected candidate, redirect chain, resolution evidence, and operator decision.
